title: Node HTTP Primitives, Connections, Protocols, Timeouts, and Shutdown · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but broken Node HTTP server protocol and timeout budget; preserve the failing exchange, locate the first causal Node or Express boundary, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in Node HTTP server protocol and timeout budget and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace IncomingMessage and ServerResponse as streams with header commitment, body ownership, backpressure, abort, error, and close lifetimes.
Configure requestTimeout, headersTimeout, keepAliveTimeout, keepAliveTimeoutBuffer, maxHeadersCount, and maximum header size from a threat and latency budget.
Distinguish HTTP/1.1, Node HTTP/2 compatibility behavior, ALPN, TLS, upgrades, CONNECT, and the explicit external boundary for HTTP/3.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Order the regression proof · Debug Protocol Transfer Evidence
steps.instruction:
Order the Protocol Transfer Evidence Node and Express server HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Order the regression proof · Debug Protocol Transfer Evidence.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Protocol Transfer Evidence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Node and Express server Order the regression proof · Debug Protocol Transfer Evidence, keep Protocol Transfer Evidence connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: A debug team handles case 4705 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate protocol support with explicit header, request, and drain policy; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Require live TLS identity, proxy, slow-client, protocol negotiation, upgrade, drain, and packet-level evidence beyond deterministic browser models.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Protocol Transfer Evidence has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Node and Express server Order the regression proof · Debug Protocol Transfer Evidence, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Protocol Transfer Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Require live TLS identity, proxy, slow-client, protocol negotiation, upgrade, drain, and packet-level evidence beyond deterministic browser models.

For Node and Express server Order the regression proof · Debug Protocol Transfer Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A pure request-decision function proves listener and wire-protocol behavior.

options.text: Declare Node and Express server method semantics, target authority, credentials, body ownership, and byte and time budgets for the debug Protocol Transfer Evidence case
Predict Node and Express server resolution, transport, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup outcomes for the debug Protocol Transfer Evidence case
Run the smallest deterministic Node and Express server request or response model and retain inspectable evidence for the debug Protocol Transfer Evidence case
Inject one changed Node and Express server failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-network transfer limits for the debug Protocol Transfer Evidence case

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate protocol support with explicit header, request, and drain policy; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Require live TLS identity, proxy, slow-client, protocol negotiation, upgrade, drain, and packet-level evidence beyond deterministic browser models.

For Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Protocol Transfer Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A pure request-decision function proves listener and wire-protocol behavior.
